title: Crispy Mexican Courgette Quesadilla
description: A festive and juicy gluten‑free quesadilla that caramelizes courgettes with cumin and chipotle for a savory burst, folded in soft corn tortillas and finished with a zesty lime kick.

ingredients.name: Gluten‑free corn tortillas
ingredients.notes: young, soft tortillas are best for folding

ingredients.name: Shredded Mexican blend cheese
ingredients.notes: Mexican blend of cheddar and Monterey Jack

instructions: Preheat a large non‑stick skillet over medium heat.

Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in the skillet and add the sliced courgettes and onions. Season with salt, pepper, cumin, and chipotle powder. Sauté, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are caramelized and the courgettes are tender, about 6–7 minutes.
While the vegetables cook, warm the tortillas: place each on the hot skillet for 20–30 seconds per side, just until they soften and develop a slight golden touch.

Once the courgette mixture is ready, remove it from the skillet. Divide it evenly among 4 tortillas, spreading it across half of each tortilla. Top with shredded cheese, sprinkle chopped cilantro, and drizzle a few drops of lime juice.

Fold each tortilla in half, covering the filling, and press gently to seal. Return the folded quesadillas to the skillet, cooking 2–3 minutes per side or until the tortillas are crispy and the cheese has melted.
Remove the finished quesadillas from the skillet and cut them into wedges. Serve immediately with extra lime wedges and a side of salsa or guacamole if desired.
